_comment: TRUE POSITIVE — SP-810001: Account Takeover Kill Chain (Impossible Travel → MFA Device Change → Mail Forwarding Rule)
scenario: Full ATO chain: attacker uses stolen credentials from overseas, changes MFA device to maintain access, creates mail forwarding rule for persistent exfiltration

S1_TRAVEL_ANOMALY._note: Successful login from Nigeria — user's last known location was Frankfurt 6 hours ago (speed: 2800 km/h — impossible)

S2_MFA_CHANGE._note: Attacker removes existing MFA device to prevent account recovery

S2_MFA_CHANGE_ADD._note: New MFA device registered from attacker's IP

S3_MAIL_RULE._note: Mail forwarding rule silently redirects all CFO mail to attacker's Gmail and deletes originals

why_tp: Impossible travel: Frankfurt to Nigeria in 6 hours (2800 km/h impossible for commercial travel)
MFA device deleted and new device registered within 8 minutes of suspicious login
Mail forwarding rule to external Gmail created 10 minutes later
All 3 stages from the same source IP
analyst_action: IMMEDIATE: Revoke all sessions for cfo@contoso.com
Remove the mail forwarding rule created at 14:18
Reset credentials and force re-enrollment of MFA from trusted location
Contact CFO to confirm the legitimate location and whether they traveled
Review all emails forwarded to attacker_collector@gmail.com for data classification
Check if any financial transactions were initiated from the compromised session
